_metadata.purpose: Evaluate existing memory systems for AI assistants to inform our design
_metadata.note: Storage efficiency measured as storage consumed per 1000 memory entries including metadata and indices

competitors.name: MemGPT
competitors.description: Virtual context management system that pages memory in and out of the LLM context window, inspired by OS virtual memory
weaknesses: Requires cloud API for LLM calls
Complex paging logic can be unpredictable

competitors.name: LangChain ConversationMemory
competitors.description: Conversation memory modules for LangChain framework, including buffer, summary, and entity memory types
weaknesses: In-memory default loses data on restart
Summary memory depends on LLM calls

competitors.name: Semantic Kernel Memory
competitors.description: Microsoft's AI orchestration framework with pluggable memory connectors supporting various vector databases
weaknesses: Storage efficiency varies widely by backend

competitors.name: LocalMind
competitors.description: Open-source local-first memory system with working, episodic, and semantic layers stored entirely on the local filesystem
strengths: Three-layer architecture similar to our design goals
Good semantic search with local embeddings
weaknesses: Embedding model must be downloaded separately

key_findings: Most existing systems depend on cloud services for either LLM calls or storage backends
LocalMind is the closest to our requirements but lacks distillation capabilities
No existing system combines all three of: local-first, three-layer memory, and automatic distillation
Storage efficiency varies significantly — our target should be under 200 MB per 1000 entries
design_implications: We should study LocalMind's architecture as a starting point
Distillation pipeline is our key differentiator
Must avoid cloud dependencies that other systems rely on
Markdown + SQLite hybrid storage appears to be the emerging pattern for local-first systems
